conversations.title: Power of Ideas
conversations.subtitle: Section A, 1b and 1c · Conversation 1

lines.speaker: guide_1
lines.text: Welcome to the International Exhibition of Inventions! Over here, we have Super See from Germany.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: They look like sunglasses. What are they used for?

lines.speaker: guide_1
lines.text: They're for people who can't see well. They don't find normal glasses helpful.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: How do they help people to see?

lines.speaker: guide_1
lines.text: Well, a mini-camera is connected to the front of the glasses. It makes everything look larger. Words can also be turned into sound.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: Cool! So they can help people watch TV and read books.

lines.speaker: guide_1
lines.text: Yes. They can also control the colour and picture.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: Amazing!

conversations.instruction: Listen at the inventions exhibition. What are Super See glasses used for?

questions.prompt: Who are Super See glasses for?
What can the glasses do with words?

conversations.title: Power of Ideas
conversations.subtitle: Section A, 1b and 1c · Conversation 2

lines.speaker: teng_fei
lines.text: What smells so delicious?

lines.speaker: guide_2
lines.text: Come and have a try. Smart Cook from China is cooking some food.

lines.speaker: teng_fei
lines.text: Really? How does it work?

lines.speaker: guide_2
lines.text: It's simple! Put food like carrots and onions in the cup on the top of the machine. Then press this button. All the food is cut and fried. The oil and salt are also added by the machine itself.

lines.speaker: teng_fei
lines.text: Fantastic! How many kinds of dishes can it make?

lines.speaker: guide_2
lines.text: Hundreds! It has an app with many recipes.

lines.speaker: teng_fei
lines.text: Great! With this robot, my mother can take a break from cooking.

lines.speaker: guide_2
lines.text: How nice of you to think so. I'm sure your mother will love it.

conversations.instruction: Listen about Smart Cook. How does the machine work?

questions.prompt: Where do you put the food?
How many kinds of dishes can Smart Cook make?

conversations.title: Power of Ideas
conversations.subtitle: Section A, 2a, 2b and 2c

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: Hi, Han Lin! Did you go to the International Exhibition of Inventions last weekend?

lines.speaker: han_lin
lines.text: Yes, some amazing inventions were shown from all over the world.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: What invention did you like the most?

lines.speaker: han_lin
lines.text: Oh, the 3D printer was amazing.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: han_lin
lines.text: Almost everything under the sun can be made by it.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: Really? Like what?

lines.speaker: han_lin
lines.text: Well, all kinds of everyday objects. Toys, chairs, tables, and even houses can all be made by the 3D printer.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: That's cool!

lines.speaker: han_lin
lines.text: Yeah. And in medicine for example, arms and legs, even hearts can be created by the 3D printer.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: That's great! How does it work?

lines.speaker: han_lin
lines.text: Well, first, the object is designed on a computer. Then, a file is created and sent to the 3D printer. The 3D printer reads the file, and after that, the object is produced by building up plastic.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: Is only plastic used?

lines.speaker: han_lin
lines.text: No, other materials can also be used to produce things. It can even make food.

lines.speaker: ella
lines.text: Oh, really? I'd love to try 3D-printed food!

conversations.instruction: Listen to Ella and Han Lin. What can a 3D printer make?

questions.prompt: Which everyday objects does Han Lin mention?
options: Toys, chairs, tables, and even houses

questions.prompt: What medical example does Han Lin give?
What is the first step in using a 3D printer?
options: Design the object on a computer
